org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions

## Class ProcessFunction<I,O>

  * [java.lang.Object](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")
  *     * [org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.AbstractRichFunction](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/AbstractRichFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")
    *       * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.ProcessFunction<I,O>

  * Type Parameters:
    `I` \- Type of the input elements.
    `O` \- Type of the output elements.

All Implemented Interfaces:
    [Serializable](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/io/Serializable.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.io"), [Function](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/Function.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [RichFunction](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")

Direct Known Subclasses:
    [IterateExample.Step](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/examples/iteration/IterateExample.Step.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.examples.iteration"), [LookupJoinRunner](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/runtime/operators/join/lookup/LookupJoinRunner.html> "class in org.apache.flink.table.runtime.operators.join.lookup"), [PythonConnectorUtils.RowRowMapper](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/python/util/PythonConnectorUtils.RowRowMapper.html> "class in org.apache.flink.python.util"), [SideOutputExample.Tokenizer](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/examples/sideoutput/SideOutputExample.Tokenizer.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.examples.sideoutput")

* * *




        [@PublicEvolving](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/annotation/PublicEvolving.html> "annotation in org.apache.flink.annotation")
        public abstract class ProcessFunction<I,O>
        extends [AbstractRichFunction](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/AbstractRichFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")

A function that processes elements of a stream.

For every element in the input stream [`processElement(Object, Context, Collector)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/ProcessFunction.html#processElement-I-org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.ProcessFunction.Context-org.apache.flink.util.Collector->) is invoked. This can produce zero or more elements as output. Implementations can also query the time and set timers through the provided [`ProcessFunction.Context`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/ProcessFunction.Context.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions"). For firing timers [`onTimer(long, OnTimerContext, Collector)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/ProcessFunction.html#onTimer-long-org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.ProcessFunction.OnTimerContext-org.apache.flink.util.Collector->) will be invoked. This can again produce zero or more elements as output and register further timers.

**NOTE:** Access to keyed state and timers (which are also scoped to a key) is only available if the `ProcessFunction` is applied on a `KeyedStream`.

**NOTE:** A `ProcessFunction` is always a [`RichFunction`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"). Therefore, access to the [`RuntimeContext`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RuntimeContext.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions") is always available and setup and teardown methods can be implemented. See [`RichFunction.open(org.apache.flink.configuration.Configuration)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFunction.html#open-org.apache.flink.configuration.Configuration->) and [`RichFunction.close()`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFunction.html#close-->).

See Also:
    [Serialized Form](<../../../../../../serialized-form.html#org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.ProcessFunction>)

    * ### Method Detail

      * #### processElement

            public abstract void processElement([I](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/ProcessFunction.html> "type parameter in ProcessFunction") value,
                                                [ProcessFunction.Context](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/ProcessFunction.Context.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions") ctx,
                                                [Collector](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/util/Collector.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.util")<[O](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/ProcessFunction.html> "type parameter in ProcessFunction")> out)
                                         throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Process one element from the input stream.

This function can output zero or more elements using the [`Collector`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/util/Collector.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.util") parameter and also update internal state or set timers using the [`ProcessFunction.Context`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/ProcessFunction.Context.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions") parameter.

Parameters:
    `value` \- The input value.
    `ctx` \- A [`ProcessFunction.Context`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/ProcessFunction.Context.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions") that allows querying the timestamp of the element and getting a [`TimerService`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/TimerService.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api") for registering timers and querying the time. The context is only valid during the invocation of this method, do not store it.
    `out` \- The collector for returning result values.
Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")` \- This method may throw exceptions. Throwing an exception will cause the operation to fail and may trigger recovery.
      * #### onTimer

            public void onTimer(long timestamp,
                                [ProcessFunction.OnTimerContext](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/ProcessFunction.OnTimerContext.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions") ctx,
                                [Collector](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/util/Collector.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.util")<[O](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/ProcessFunction.html> "type parameter in ProcessFunction")> out)
                         throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Called when a timer set using [`TimerService`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/TimerService.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api") fires.

Parameters:
    `timestamp` \- The timestamp of the firing timer.
    `ctx` \- An [`ProcessFunction.OnTimerContext`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/ProcessFunction.OnTimerContext.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions") that allows querying the timestamp of the firing timer, querying the [`TimeDomain`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/TimeDomain.html> "enum in org.apache.flink.streaming.api") of the firing timer and getting a [`TimerService`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/TimerService.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api") for registering timers and querying the time. The context is only valid during the invocation of this method, do not store it.
    `out` \- The collector for returning result values.
Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")` \- This method may throw exceptions. Throwing an exception will cause the operation to fail and may trigger recovery.
